items.headline: Messi magic saves Argentina — but tiny Cape Verde nearly pulled off the shock of the century
items.body: What a night in the Round of 32 finale. Lionel Messi opened the scoring with an outrageous outside-of-the-boot flick — his 20th career World Cup goal — but Cape Verde, a tiny island nation of half a million at their first-ever World Cup, refused to go quietly. They equalized twice, including a stunner from the edge of the box in extra time, before an own goal off a Messi free kick in the 111th minute finally sent the champions through 3-2. Argentina survive; the 'Blue Sharks' go home as heroes.
items.say: Argentina barely survived last night — this tiny island team, Cape Verde, took them to extra time and almost knocked out the champs. Messi scored a ridiculous one, then an own goal saved them 3-2. Wild game.

links.text: FOX Sports: Argentina 3-2 Cape Verde extended highlights
ESPN: Argentina break Cape Verde hearts in extra time

items.headline: Wimbledon stunner: 21-year-old Alex Eala dethrones defending champ Iga Swiatek
items.body: The upset of the tournament. Filipina sensation Alexandra Eala knocked out reigning champion Iga Swiatek 7-6(9), 6-2 on Centre Court, edging a nerve-shredding 11-9 first-set tiebreak before running away with it — and becoming the first Filipina ever to reach the second week of a Grand Slam. She wasn't the only big name to fall, either: 2022 champ Elena Rybakina also crashed out. The women's draw has officially been blown wide open.
items.say: Big Wimbledon shocker — a 21-year-old from the Philippines, Alex Eala, just beat the defending champion Iga Swiatek. First Filipina ever to make the second week of a Slam. Cinderella stuff.

links.text: WTA: Eala dethrones Swiatek, makes history
Olympics.com: Eala stuns Swiatek in third-round epic

items.headline: The World Cup last 16 kicks off today — France and Canada in action
items.body: The real drama starts now. The Round of 16 opens today with two beauties: co-hosts Canada take on Morocco in Houston (1pm ET), then Kylian Mbappé's France — fresh off topping their group — meet surprise-package Paraguay in Philadelphia (5pm ET). Single elimination from here on out; win and you're in the quarterfinals, lose and your summer's over. Perfect Fourth of July couch fuel.
items.say: The World Cup last 16 starts today — Canada play Morocco, then France take on Paraguay tonight. Knockout football all weekend. You throwing it on at the cookout?

links.text: ESPN: today's World Cup live updates & schedule
FOX Sports: full Round of 16 bracket

items.headline: The Tour de France rolls out of Barcelona today — first Grand Départ ever in Spain
items.body: Cycling's biggest race begins today with a first: the Grand Départ in Barcelona, a punchy 19.6km team time trial that whips past the Sagrada Família before a double climb up Montjuïc to the line. The first yellow jersey is up for grabs, and defending champ Tadej Pogačar — chasing a record-equalling fifth title — starts last with two-time winner Jonas Vingegaard lurking. Three weeks and 3,300+ km to Paris start right here.
items.say: The Tour de France starts today — first time it's ever kicked off in Barcelona. Pogačar's going for a fifth win. Great lazy-weekend background watching.

links.text: Olympics.com: 2026 Tour schedule & live stage results
Domestique: Stage 1 team time trial preview

items.headline: MLB reveals its All-Star starters today — Ohtani headlines the show
items.body: The Midsummer Classic starting lineups drop today on FOX, and Shohei Ohtani leads the way — he ran away with the fan vote to start at DH for the National League. The full slate of starters, pitchers and reserves fills in this afternoon ahead of the July 14 game at Citizens Bank Park in Philadelphia. One bummer for the American League: injured stars Aaron Judge and Mike Trout are both racing the clock to be fit in time.
items.say: They're announcing the baseball All-Star starters today — Ohtani got the most votes again, obviously. Game's July 14 in Philly. You think your guy made it?

links.text: Yahoo Sports: Ohtani earns automatic All-Star start
ESPN: 2026 All-Star Weekend — schedule & how to watch

items.headline: Miki Sudo eats her way to a 12th title as Joey Chestnut chases No. 18 at Coney Island
items.body: The most American 12 minutes of the year delivered again. On the women's side, Miki Sudo powered down 38.5 hot dogs to win her 12th Mustard Belt and fifth straight crown. Over on the men's side, 18-time legend Joey 'Jaws' Chestnut is back at Coney Island going for title No. 18 (his absurd record is 76 dogs and buns in 10 minutes). It's the perfect ridiculous background watch for your cookout.
items.say: Nathan's hot dog contest is on today — Miki Sudo already won her 12th title, and Joey Chestnut's going for his 18th. The guy once ate 76 hot dogs in ten minutes. Peak Fourth of July.

links.text: Yahoo Sports: Sudo wins 12th title, Chestnut takes the stage

items.headline: Snow Patrol and Kylie Minogue team up on the summery new single 'These Alarms'
items.body: An unexpected pairing that just works. Snow Patrol frontman Gary Lightbody wrote 'These Alarms' with Kylie Minogue in mind — it even carried the working title 'KYLIE' — and now the pop icon has actually sung it, a shimmering, synth-driven singalong that's tailor-made for a summer road trip. It's a warm, breezy earworm from two acts your whole office will recognize.
items.say: Snow Patrol did a song with Kylie Minogue — 'These Alarms.' Kind of a random combo but it's a proper summer earworm. Give it a spin.

links.text: Hotpress: Snow Patrol release new single with Kylie

items.headline: 'The Bear' is back for its final season — all eight episodes are streaming now
items.body: One of TV's most beloved (and most stressful) shows is winding down. The fifth and final season of 'The Bear' is out on Hulu, and it picks up with Sydney taking charge of the restaurant after Carmy walks away — the crew scrambling, short on cash and battling a storm, to pull off one last perfect service and finally chase down a Michelin star. If you've been holding off, this is the weekend to binge it.
items.say: The Bear's final season is out — all eight episodes dropped on Hulu. If you loved the chaos of that kitchen, clear your weekend. Everyone's going to be talking about the ending.

links.text: Disney+: The Bear Season 5 now streaming

items.headline: The Minions take over the holiday — 'Minions & Monsters' rules the July 4 box office
items.body: The little yellow guys own the weekend. Illumination's 'Minions & Monsters' — in which the Minions conquer old-timey Hollywood, lose it all, and accidentally unleash a monster apocalypse — is out-earning the field for the Fourth of July, tracking for a $65M+ opening. It's the go-to family matinee if you're wrangling kids over the long weekend.
items.say: The new Minions movie is running away with the July 4 box office. Perfect excuse to duck into the AC and take the kids to the movies this weekend.

links.text: Boxoffice Pro: Minions & Monsters set to lead the weekend
